summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a chill show highlighted by an incendiary Althea and strong early set, but hampered later by Garcia’s waning voice and divisive Vince keyboards.
review: The SBD source is clear and listenable, letting the first set shine with a smoking Althea, crisp Cold Rain & Snow, and vibrant Black Peter. While reviewers love the relaxed vibe and Vince Welnick’s “Way to Go Home” solo, they note Jerry Garcia’s vocal dip in the latter half and mixed feelings about the new digital keyboards, giving the performance an overall mixed but enjoyable feel.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the recording quality (good‑to‑excellent) and early set performance, but several point out vocal weaknesses and keyboard controversy, leading to a solid yet not outstanding overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the SBD "good quality" and cite clear sound; no major drop‑outs or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as "smokin" and energetic, while the second set attempts a build‑up that many felt never fully materialized, giving the concert a laid‑back, chill atmosphere overall.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Strong start with great voice and licks; later half struggles with vocal fatigue and less inspired playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Consistently solid throughout the night, praised as "on the whole night".
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince Welnick’s digital piano draws polarized reactions – some love his energetic solo "Way to Go Home," others criticize the shrill Casio sound.
